turns.text: Q4 2024 was exceptional. We added 19 million paid subscribers — the largest quarterly net add in our history — to reach 301.6 million globally, exceeding our 300 million target. Revenue was $10.25 billion, up 16% year-over-year. The ad-supported plan now represents 55% of new sign-ups in our ad markets. Live sports and events are proving to be powerful acquisition tools, and we will add more live programming including NFL Christmas games in 2025.

Q4 operating income was $2.27 billion at a 22% margin, as guided. Full year 2024 operating margin was 26%, on target. For 2025, we are guiding to revenue of $43 to $44 billion and an operating margin of 29%, at the top of our prior guidance range. We expect to generate more than $8 billion in free cash flow in 2025. We will resume share repurchases in Q1 2025.
Our next question comes from Mark Mahaney at Evercore ISI.
Greg, 19 million adds in a quarter is extraordinary. How much of that is attributable to live events versus the continued password-sharing enforcement versus the ad-supported pricing attractiveness?

statements.claim: Netflix added 19 million paid subscribers in Q4 2024, the largest quarterly net add in company history.
Full year 2025 revenue guidance is $43 to $44 billion with a 29% operating margin.
Netflix expects to generate more than $8 billion in free cash flow in 2025.
Netflix reached 301.6 million global paid subscribers in Q4 2024, exceeding the 300 million target.
The ad-supported plan now represents 55% of new sign-ups in Netflix's ad markets.
